text: Following the completion of the lymphadenectomy, the assistant applies traction to the colon, revealing the Douglas pouch for further examination.
The surgeon identifies the vas deferens and seminal vesicles within the posterior plane, highlighting these anatomical structures during the procedure.
The surgeon continues the posterior dissection along the posterior aspect of the prostate, carefully separating tissue in this anatomical region.
The surgeon controls the vascular pedicles using either a vessel sealer or hemlock clips, ensuring secure management of these structures during the procedure.
